reviews.author: Chetan
reviews.text: We rode bicycles to the beach from our hotel near the Old Quarter. It took us 45 minutes, but it was definitely worth it. The views along the way were amazing; we saw green grass fields and local farming. People were relaxing in the grass, and we even saw a lady helping a farmer feed a cow, which was fun to watch. We also saw local fishermen bringing in their morning catch in round basket boats. Overall, it was a great ride, and the coconut water was delicious.

reviews.author: Dimitri Sterlin
reviews.text: It's a nice beach with fine sand. I went there during week days so it wasn't busy. The beach is clean and water temperature is perfect to cool down. There are a few vendors trying to sell you stuff but they are very polite and might have a quick chat with you.

reviews.author: Nikolay Manolov
reviews.text: The beach is great for a city one. Sunbeds cost 75k, there's restaurants and bars nearby. The view is pretty nice as well. The beach gets a little crowded, especially under the umbrellas, so keep that in mind if you artive late.

reviews.author: Dr. D.
reviews.text: We really like this beach and the people who use it, but as of today, there are illegal jet skis and boats here. They react aggressively to comments. They're interfering with swimming on the public beach, there's spilled gasoline, there's trash. We called the police twice, and we weren't the only ones. No one came. If you're looking for a safe place to relax, look elsewhere or wait until these people leave.

reviews.author: Russ Lacuata
reviews.text: Clean, beautiful stretch of beach. Warm water, soft sand, and calm waves perfect for the whole family. Plenty of good restaurants lined along the beach, if you order food from them then you dont have to pay the lounger and umbrella. We are lucky that we are staying just a good 5 minutes walk away from the beach. During our stay, we get to do our early morning walk and swim in the morning.

reviews.author: Sarah
reviews.text: Nice beach if you find the right spot. Nice sand, relatively clean, warm sea with a reasonable current to be aware of. Seemed to be a life guard on beach. We found it easiest to pick a nice cafe bar and use that as a base, showers available at different cafes. Lots of parking lots trying to tell us we weren't allowed to cycle through, just keep moving until you find a quiet spot to think, most beach cafes seem to have their own free parking once you got to them.

reviews.author: HY Dong
reviews.text: An Bang Beach is beautifully maintained and one of the cleanest beaches I’ve visited in Vietnam. The sand is soft and spotless, and there are plenty of sun loungers available if you want to relax by the sea. I came right after a rain shower, so it was peaceful and uncrowded — the perfect time to enjoy the sound of the waves and the fresh ocean air. A wonderful spot to unwind and escape the busy streets of Hoi An. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: NTtravels
reviews.text: The place looks very nice! I visited during winter, so I can't comment extensively on what it's like in other seasons, but overall it made a great impression. One thing to note: there's a parking fee if you arrive by bicycle or motorbike.

reviews.author: Robbie Black
reviews.text: So sad to see the storm damage and beach washed away, really feel for the locals making an effort to stay in business, so resilient 💖 A few derelict and abandoned resorts, suggest 1-2 nights maybe, not resort package deals, the big resorts like The Pearl were essentially ghost towns when we walked the area early Dec 2025. Pics and videos attached speak volumes... 😓 The determined locals will find a way to bring back, long extensive process though 💪❤️

reviews.author: Ankita Arya
reviews.text: I had a wonderful experience in An Beach ! The place is absolutely beautiful, spotlessly clean, and peaceful. The water is stunning, and I visited during evening , so the weather was perfect and the cleanliness was impressive. The crowd was minimal, making it even more enjoyable. Overall, it was a fantastic and relaxing experience!

reviews.author: Holli Scott
reviews.text: Local beach access where you can rent beach chairs and umbrellas for 50 vnd about $2 and towels for 30 vnd. Drink and food services available. Great people watching and watersports. Catch a grab for a ride if needed.

reviews.author: Pied Piper
reviews.text: An bang beach and the next beach along cua dai are full of rubbish and stuffs to keep further erosion (that they caused), and are still causing, by over construction at bay. It will not work, and a tsunami, or even just powerful waves will wash this away. Only good thing was watching the fisherman dredge molluscs from the seabed. Which actually also is very damaging for the coast. Huge JCB'S were further extracting sand and sediment from large coastal areas along the beach. They clearly have not learned from past mistakes.

reviews.author: James Harris
reviews.text: Great beach, free bike parking and a very cheap Banh Mi at “Win Win” restaurant just off the main road. Sea was lovely and the waves fun. We didn’t hire sunbeds but did have a smoothie at one of the beachside restaurants.

reviews.author: Chris Edwards
reviews.text: A bit to congested with loungers and chairs but everything else was good. Sea was clean and on the day I went there lit had some nice big waves which are ok for me as I am a strong swimmer.

reviews.author: Lydia Chu
reviews.text: An Bang Beach in Hoi An is a beautiful and relaxing spot with soft sand and clear water. It’s a great place to swim, walk, or just unwind by the sea. There are plenty of beachside cafes and restaurants where you can grab a drink or meal while enjoying the view. It can get a bit crowded later in the day, but the atmosphere is laid-back and friendly. A perfect place to spend a sunny afternoon in Hoi An.

reviews.author: Thủy Tiên Vương
reviews.text: A beautiful white-sand beach with gentle waves. Lots of seashells for kids to pick up, and several seafood restaurants along the shore. It’s best to avoid the rainy or winter season if you want to swim and sunbathe all day, though we were lucky to visit on a sunny winter day.

reviews.author: Tzvi Arden
reviews.text: Wide, expansive golden sands meet calm waters (not the clearest, but overall fine)—perfect for relaxing strolls. Super pleasant vibe with tons of beachfront restaurants offering fresh eats right on the sand. Rented a surfboard easily and had a blast catching waves. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Gaurav Agrawal
reviews.text: One of the pristine beaches located around 5 km from hoi an You can reach here by grab or can rent a bi cycle The beach sand is as near as white Various caffès are available at the beach People flocked here for sun bathing Sun loungers are available here at rent There is lack of public toilet/change room No ticket is required Thus places is so beautiful that you can relax here for hours

reviews.author: Sowmya Sreeram
reviews.text: Beautiful beach. There is a Vegan Restaurant near by this beach - "The Fisherman Vegan". Beachside chairs and umbrella can be rented. We paid 400,000 for 3 chairs.

reviews.author: David EB
reviews.text: Very nice and cleaner than I thought from reading reviews. Very very long walkable beach with sand. Sand can get very hot so make sure to bring footwear. Otherwise the water is nice and clear and you do not need water shoes. Not much to snorkel here but if you have goggles there are some different fish to see as well as occasional jellyfish… The beach chairs and umbrellas are free if you order food and drinks from the restaurants which own those chairs…

reviews.author: Dave Mariani
reviews.text: The shoreline has lots of garbage if you r not at a beach operation or restaurant. We walked for a few miles on the beach and felt safe, it just had a lot of garbage.

reviews.author: Victor Carpinelli
reviews.text: This place offers a refreshing escape from the city. There are numerous restaurants and bars that provide beach service, with some being free and others not. While the beach itself may not be particularly spectacular, it serves its purpose. I visited in January, and the water was quite cold. To get in and out, you can use Grab, or if you’re feeling adventurous, rent a scooter.

reviews.author: Elizabeth Fowler
reviews.text: So sad to see this beach, being washed away bit by bit. No longer a place to swim and enjoy, and businesses hanging on for dear life along the waterfront. See videos and pics 😫

reviews.author: John Paul Mellish
reviews.text: Lovely beach, kept clean outside hotels bars etc, but between this places, A lot of washed up debris

reviews.author: Martyna Przybysz
reviews.text: It’s a lovely chilled beach. The water is clear. I’d go early, as all the beach front restaurants start packing at 4pm. Sadly, no sunsets.

reviews.author: John Winter
reviews.text: A great spot to unwind, An Bang Beach offers a clean stretch of sand, plenty of shade under beach huts, and rows of loungers perfect for relaxing. The beachfront is lined with a mix of thatched umbrellas and green canvas canopies, giving it a tropical feel. There’s a safety net installed along the swimming area which is a thoughtful touch, especially on busier days. The beach was clean and pleasant to walk along barefoot. At the top of the beach, you’ll find a long line of restaurants and bars. Most of the better spots (and better prices) are on the right-hand side if you’re facing the ocean – definitely worth walking all the way down to compare. It can get quite crowded during the hotter afternoons, but that only adds to the lively vibe. Whether you’re in the mood for a swim, a sunbathe, or a chilled coconut under a hut, this beach delivers a great afternoon escape.

reviews.author: Leone Doyle
reviews.text: The water has broken cement sheet, concrete and roof tiles. You can’t relax without someone trying to sell you something. It’s cheap and better than no beach…

reviews.author: Nitin Awasthi
reviews.text: A beautiful, relaxed beach and a perfect escape from the busier tourist spots. The sand is clean, the water is great for swimming, and the overall vibe is calm and laid-back. There are plenty of beachside cafés and restaurants where you can enjoy food or drinks with a great sea view, without feeling overcrowded or rushed. It’s especially lovely in the early morning or around sunset. Overall, quite peaceful, scenic, and very easy to unwind. A great place to spend a few hours or even an entire day if you’re visiting Hoi An.

reviews.author: Pig Mouse
reviews.text: Beautiful beach with smooth powdery sand and clear seawater, relaxing vibes surrounded by plenty of seafood restos, very family-friendly. A recommended place while in Hoi An for sure !

reviews.author: Chinthan Krishna K
reviews.text: It's a very calm beach. And the water only comes up to about 5ft 6inches even 100mtrs into the water. Nice restaurants nearby and some sea side activities. There's a small castle like structure with graffiti on it too!
